lenses.guidance: 'Remember this day' (v 3) frames every subsequent observance. The chapter sits between Passover and Red Sea — the narrative installs liturgical memory before the next deliverance. Identity comes from rehearsed story.

Joseph's bones travel with Israel (v 19) — Gen 50:25's promise carried by the fleeing nation. The pattern of Joseph delivered through Egypt foreshadows Israel delivered through Egypt; both prefigure Christ.

The firstborn consecration (vv 2, 12-13) opens a thread that ends at Hebrews 12:23 ('the assembly of the firstborn enrolled in heaven') and Christ as 'firstborn of all creation' (Col 1:15). The category begins here in liturgy.

God 'did not lead them by way of the Philistines... lest the people change their minds' (v 17). Trust the longer route; God knows when faith is too young for the direct path. Today, accept detours as mercy.
